name: PR-Z 110 Sierra de Santa Cruz Loop Trail
name_original: Pinaspar de Orcajo - Ermita de San Nicolás - Mojón Blanco — trail running circular por la Sierra de Santa Cruz
description: This difficult 8.5-mile (13.7 km) jogging loop in Sierra de Santa Cruz offers historical landmarks and peak views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Orcajo offer access to diverse natural landscapes in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon, Spain. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Sierra de Santa Cruz mountain range, providing trails with varying elevations and scenic views. Nearby, the Laguna de Gallocanta presents flat, expansive routes around a significant saline lake and bird sanctuary. Additionally, the natural park surrounding Monasterio de Piedra, located approximately 25 km away, features trails amidst waterfalls and lush greenery. Best jogging routes around Orcajo The most popular jogging route is Pinaspar de Orcajo - Hermitage of San Nicolás - Mojón Blanco — trail running circular por la Sierra de Santa Cruz, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Sierra de Santa Cruz, offering varied terrain and elevation. Another top favourite among local runners is Daroca – Puerta Baja de Daroca loop from Estación de Daroca, a difficult 14.0 miles (22.5 km) path. This longer route features significant elevation gain through the area surrounding Daroca. Local runners also love the Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Daroca, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through the historic surroundings of Daroca, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Jogging around Orcajo is defined by mountain ranges like Sierra de Santa Cruz, expansive lake shores at Laguna de Gallocanta, and natural park trails with waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. Around 30 runners have used komoot to explore Orcajo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Orcajo?
A: The region around Orcajo offers a diverse range of terrain for runners. You can find trails with varying elevations in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Santa Cruz&lt;/strong&gt;, providing a challenging mountain running experience. Alternatively, the areas near the &lt;strong&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta&lt;/strong&gt; offer flatter, more expansive routes, ideal for those seeking a less strenuous run with unique ecological scenery. Some routes also pass through historic surroundings, like those near Daroca.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate jogging routes available near Orcajo?
A: While many routes in the area are considered difficult, there are options for moderate runs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.8 km trail that takes you through the historic surroundings of Daroca. This offers a good option for those looking for a less challenging run compared to the more demanding mountain trails.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in the Orcajo area?
A: Yes, there are several longer running trails available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca – Puerta Baja de Daroca loop from Estación de Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 22.5 km path with significant elevation gain, perfect for endurance runners. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Estación de Daroca&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 15.7 km.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Orcajo?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983211607/pinaspar-de-orcajo-hermitage-of-san-nicolas-mojon-blanco-trail-running-circular-por-la-sierra-de-santa-cruz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinaspar de Orcajo - Hermitage of San Nicolás - Mojón Blanco — trail running circular por la Sierra de Santa Cruz&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 13.7 km trail exploring the Sierra de Santa Cruz. Other circular options include loops around Daroca, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de San Cristóbal loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or points of interest can I see while jogging near Orcajo?
A: The region boasts several natural and historical points of interest. You can run near the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, a vital wetland and bird sanctuary. Approximately 25 km away, the &lt;strong&gt;Monasterio de Piedra&lt;/strong&gt; natural park offers stunning waterfalls and lush greenery. Closer to Orcajo, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Santa Cruz&lt;/strong&gt; provides scenic mountain views. Historic sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca&lt;/a&gt; and its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible on some routes.

Q: Is Orcajo a good destination for family-friendly running or walking activities?
A: While many of the listed running routes are rated as difficult, the diverse landscape around Orcajo does offer potential for family-friendly activities. The flat, expansive areas around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; could be suitable for easier walks or runs with children, offering opportunities for birdwatching. For specific family-friendly routes, it's recommended to check individual trail details for elevation and terrain suitability.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails around Orcajo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in Aragon, but it's always best to check specific regulations for natural parks or protected areas like the Laguna de Gallocanta, which is a bird sanctuary. When running with your dog, ensure they are kept under control, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and always clean up after them. For specific routes, look for local signage or information regarding pet policies.

Q: What is the best season to go jogging in Orcajo?
A: The best season for jogging in Orcajo largely depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for exploring both mountain trails and lakeside paths. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winters are generally mild but can be chilly, particularly in the mountains, so appropriate gear is essential.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls near Orcajo?
A: While not directly within Orcajo, the natural park surrounding &lt;strong&gt;Monasterio de Piedra&lt;/strong&gt;, located approximately 25 km away, is renowned for its stunning waterfalls, lush greenery, and intricate cave systems. If you're willing to travel a short distance, this area offers exceptional jogging opportunities amidst breathtaking scenery, including numerous cascades and grottoes.

Q: What are the parking options for accessing running routes in Orcajo?
A: As Orcajo is a small municipality, parking is generally available within the village itself. For routes starting from nearby towns like Daroca, there are usually designated parking areas. When planning your run, it's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for specific parking recommendations or nearby facilities.

Q: Can I reach the running trails around Orcajo using public transport?
A: Orcajo itself is a small, rural village, and direct public transport links to specific trailheads might be limited. However, larger nearby towns like Daroca may have bus connections. From Daroca, some running routes are accessible. For detailed public transport information, it's best to consult local bus schedules or regional transport services for connections to Orcajo or surrounding areas.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Orcajo area?
A: There are several dedicated running routes documented for the Orcajo area on komoot, with a total of 6 tours currently listed. These routes offer a variety of distances and difficulties, catering to different preferences for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.
tips: The Peirón de la Virgen del Pilar in Orcajo is one of the most unique peirones in the region. Located at the fork in the road to the Santed mountain pass, which leads to some fields.

The Hermitage of San Nicolás de Orcajo, also known as the Hermitage of San Cristóbal, is a place of worship located in Orcajo, in the province of Zaragoza. The hermitage is located near the peak of Cerro del Verdugal and Cerro de la Almenara Alta.
Pico del Mojón Blanco is a prominent peak in the Sierra de Santa Cruz, near Balconchán, Aragon. Pico del Mojón Blanco offers spectacular views of Laguna de Gallocanta and the surrounding area.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the PR-Z 110 Sierra de Santa Cruz Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is designed for jogging and typically takes around 1 hour and 49 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging trail?
faqs.answer: The PR-Z 110 Sierra de Santa Cruz Loop Trail is rated as difficult. It features a significant elevation gain of approximately 434 meters over its 13.74-kilometer distance, making it suitable for experienced joggers with good fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: While specific terrain details for this exact trail are not widely documented, 'PR' trails in Spain often feature varied surfaces including dirt paths, rocky sections, and sometimes paved or gravel roads. Given its 'difficult' rating and elevation changes, expect some challenging and uneven sections.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You'll encounter the Peirón de Orcajo, the historic Ermita de San Nicolás de Orcajo, and the scenic Mojón Blanco Peak, which likely offers excellent views.

faqs.question: Is the PR-Z 110 Sierra de Santa Cruz Loop Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this particular trail are not available, many 'PR' trails in Spain are generally dog-friendly, often requiring dogs to be on a leash. It's always best to check local signage at the trailhead for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Given that 'PR' trails are common in Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for jogging, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold. Summer can be very hot, especially in inland areas, and winter might bring colder temperatures or even snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking or trailhead access for this route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour starts near Pinaspar de Orcajo, suggesting that parking might be available in or near this village. Look for designated parking areas or safe roadside spots close to the start of the loop. Specific trailhead access details would typically be found on local signage.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: Most 'PR' (Pequeño Recorrido) trails in Spain do not require permits or entrance fees for public access. However, it's always advisable to check for any local regulations or protected area requirements upon arrival, especially if the trail passes through a natural park.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as 'difficult' and involves a significant distance and elevation gain. It is not recommended for beginners and requires a very good fitness level and sure-footedness.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Pinaspar de Orcajo. Smaller villages in Spain often have basic amenities such as a local bar or restaurant, and sometimes guesthouses or rural accommodation. For more extensive options, you might need to travel to a larger nearby town.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and length, you should bring plenty of water, appropriate trail running shoes, weather-appropriate clothing (layers are often best), snacks, a fully charged phone, and a map or GPS device (like Komoot) for navigation.
